What are Marek Horváth's views on humane slaughter?

Marek Horváth considers "humane slaughter" to be an oxymoron and rejects the concept entirely. As a food ethics philosopher at Charles University in Prague, he maintains that all animal agriculture is inherently immoral, regardless of welfare standards. He argues that there is no ethical way to kill sentient beings for food.

How does Marek Horváth view the moral status of eating meat?

Marek Horváth holds that eating meat is morally equivalent to participating in a system of mass violence against sentient beings. This represents one of his core professional positions as a food ethics philosopher. He does not distinguish between different types of meat consumption or production methods in this moral assessment.

What is Marek Horváth's position on dairy farming practices?

Marek Horváth views dairy farming as cruel exploitation of animals. He specifically argues that practices like forcibly impregnating cows and separating calves from their mothers are indefensible. His position is that the dairy industry fundamentally relies on suffering, regardless of the welfare standards implemented.

What organization did Marek Horváth found, and when?

Marek Horváth founded "Rostliny Pro Život" (Plants for Life) in 2017, a Prague-based vegan advocacy organization. At 44 years old, he continues his work as a food ethics philosopher at Charles University while leading this advocacy group. The organization promotes his views on animal rights and plant-based living.

His position demands complete abstention from animal products rather than reduction.
